core_skills: C++ (11+), Python, Embedded systems, Computer vision, Object detection
skill_match_scores: C++ (11+):0.0, Python:1.0, Embedded systems:0.0, Computer vision:0.0, Object detection:0.0
must_have_quals: Bachelor's degree in computer science, software engineering, or a related field, 0-2 years of demonstrated experience in software development, Experience writing C++ (11+) and/or Python applications for embedded systems, Experience building and evaluating computer vision models for object detection and recognition, Solid understanding of design patterns, data structures, and advanced programming techniques, Excellent verbal and written communication skills in a multiple internal and external stakeholder environment, Strong analytical skills that enable data-driven decisions and prioritization, Experience with soft real-time and multi-threaded systems
preferred_quals: Experience designing and developing robotic systems using robotics frameworks, including ROS, and existing libraries and tools, Experience with path planning, AI algorithms, navigation algorithms, or robot behavior systems, Experience with continuous integration, deployment and release management tools, Experience with Agile and Scrum methodology, Desire to constantly learn, receive and act on feedback, and share knowledge
core_responsibilities: Build and review performance metrics and dashboards of the production and/or pilot fleet as a key indicator of customer experience, and product performance, Analyze trends and identify recommendations in product performance for improvements in our model training pipeline and operations, Develop code and deliver software features/functionality that increases customer value and product scalability, Increase the agility/ software release frequency of the team and organization through code refactoring, simplification, and removal, Coordinate with cross-functional areas of expertise including hardware, firmware, platform, user experience, quality, cloud, autonomy and Applied ML engineering to deliver world-class customer value, Remain up to date with developments across platform, AI, autonomy, navigation, and other robots, in order to leverage and contribute towards software innovation across all products
job_summary: Brain Corp is a San Diego, California-based AI company creating transformative core technology for the robotics industry. The Software Engineer I will work on pilots of a newly launched product, focusing on solving complex problems in retail automation and improving product performance through software development and collaboration with cross-functional teams.
